name: Core Terrain Rules
description: Authoritative gameplay, material, and generation notes for terrain IDs. Biome display names live in i18n catalogs.

rules.realWorldDescription: Deep ocean is abyssal water beyond the continental shelf, usually low elevation, far from land, and shaped by trenches, currents, and open-water circulation.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Acts as a major world barrier, long-range navigation space, and habitat for pelagic or abyssal creatures.

generation.possibility: High when the sea-level target is high or far from land; reduced in land-heavy worlds.

surfaceEffects.effect: higher wave texture and rougher navigation
partial ice cover that slows ships and blocks small craft

physicalBehavior.movement: Impassable on foot; ships or flight required.
physicalBehavior.control: Water drift and storm currents can push movement off course.

methods.notes: Only world-scale magic or tectonic edits can raise abyssal water.

rules.realWorldDescription: Open ocean is navigable marine water beyond coastal shallows, strongly influenced by currents, winds, and distance from shore.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Supports sea travel routes, island discovery, fishing grounds, and naval encounters.

generation.possibility: Common in most seeds; varies with the procedural ocean ratio.
conditions: not shallow enough for reef or shelf

physicalBehavior.movement: Impassable on foot; medium ship speed.
physicalBehavior.control: Currents and storms reduce steering.

rules.realWorldDescription: Shallow sea is sunlit continental shelf water near land, often rich in sediment, kelp, shoals, and marine life.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Forms coastal approach zones, safer boat travel, and good fishing or reef transition areas.

generation.possibility: Common along coastlines and island shelves.

surfaceEffects.effect: pale shoal texture and partial foot crossing later

physicalBehavior.movement: Blocked on foot unless a ford/sandbar system allows it; fast for small boats.
physicalBehavior.control: Reefs and shoals require careful steering.

methods.notes: Delta or barrier systems can turn shallows into shore.

rules.realWorldDescription: Coasts include beaches, tidal flats, barrier strands, cliffs, and the immediate land-water boundary.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Prime settlement, port, road-end, ship landing, and amphibious encounter terrain.

generation.possibility: Always appears where land meets ocean or lake margins.

surfaceEffects.effect: darker damp edge and slower carts

physicalBehavior.movement: Easy on foot; roads and carts work well unless flooded.
physicalBehavior.control: Wet sand can slightly reduce control.

methods.notes: Dunes and backshore become dry land.

rules.realWorldDescription: Lakes are inland water bodies in basins, glacial depressions, volcanic calderas, or river floodplains.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Freshwater hub for towns, wildlife, fishing, and future river/drainage systems.

generation.possibility: Appears when low water is disconnected from the world-edge ocean.

surfaceEffects.effect: thin ice layer that may become walkable later
green surface tint and creature change

physicalBehavior.movement: Blocked on foot; boats or swimming gear required.
physicalBehavior.control: Calmer than ocean but mud edges slow exits.

methods.notes: Lake retreats into saturated low ground.

rules.realWorldDescription: Lowlands are low-relief plains and basins, commonly fertile and easy for roads, farms, and dense settlement.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Best early-game travel, farming, towns, and road-network terrain.

generation.possibility: Common on land-heavy seeds with moderate climate values.

surfaceEffects.effect: slower carts and visible wet texture

physicalBehavior.movement: Fastest wild-land walking and best road building.
physicalBehavior.control: Stable except in mud.

methods.notes: Farmable plain becomes open dry grass.

rules.realWorldDescription: Grasslands are open herbaceous regions with limited tree cover, often controlled by rainfall, grazing, and fire.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Readable travel terrain with roaming creatures, villages, and good mounted movement.

generation.possibility: Common fallback land when moisture and elevation are moderate.

physicalBehavior.movement: Fast foot and mounted movement.
physicalBehavior.control: Stable; tall grass may hide obstacles.

methods.notes: Moist protected grassland succeeds to forest.

rules.realWorldDescription: Forests are tree-dominated ecosystems, usually needing enough moisture and a climate that supports woody growth.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Dense exploration terrain for concealment, wood resources, animals, and slower roads.

generation.possibility: Common in moist mid-temperature lands.

surfaceEffects.effect: white canopy highlights and slower passage

physicalBehavior.movement: Moderately slow off-road; roads cut movement cost strongly.
physicalBehavior.control: Reduced line of sight and tighter turning.

methods.notes: Forest opens into grass or scrub.

rules.realWorldDescription: Deserts are arid regions with very low precipitation, sparse vegetation, dunes, gravel plains, salt pans, or rocky flats.
rules.alohayoBehavior: High-risk travel terrain with heat, thirst, sand movement, and rare oasis targets.

generation.possibility: Appears where heat is high and moisture is very low.

surfaceEffects.effect: moving dune highlights and softened roads

physicalBehavior.movement: Slow on sand; roads and mounts help.
physicalBehavior.control: Loose ground reduces acceleration and turning.

methods.notes: Local water creates a green pocket.

rules.realWorldDescription: Wetlands are saturated soils near floodplains, lake margins, deltas, or groundwater discharge zones.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Slow but resource-rich lowland with water birds, reeds, mud, and settlement edges.

generation.possibility: Appears in moist low elevations near water or flooded basins.

surfaceEffects.effect: dark mud layer and heavy slow

physicalBehavior.movement: Slow off-road; roads need raised causeways.
physicalBehavior.control: Sticky mud reduces control.

methods.notes: Persistent water turns it into open freshwater.

rules.realWorldDescription: Highlands are elevated broken terrain above plains but below major alpine zones, with ridges, moors, and rough grazing.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Transition terrain between plains and mountains, with passes, wind exposure, and scattered settlements.

generation.possibility: Common around mountain belts and elevated interiors.

surfaceEffects.effect: pale edge highlights and slippery control

physicalBehavior.movement: Slower than plains; roads and passes matter.
physicalBehavior.control: Uneven slope reduces control.

methods.notes: Long erosion softens upland into lower relief.
Tectonic edit raises highland into mountain.

rules.realWorldDescription: Bare rock is exposed bedrock, scree, lava, or thin-soil slope where plants struggle to establish.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Hard, low-cover, resource-bearing terrain with poor roads but mineral interest.

generation.possibility: Appears in dry or rugged uplands and mountain shoulders.

surfaceEffects.effect: loose stone texture and sliding risk

physicalBehavior.movement: Slow and noisy; climbing gear helps.
physicalBehavior.control: Slippery or loose under weather effects.
entryRequirements: climbing gear recommended for steep routes

methods.notes: Weathered rock gains enough soil for upland vegetation.

rules.realWorldDescription: Mountains are high, rugged relief with steep slopes, ridges, cliffs, passes, and strong elevation climate gradients.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Major barrier, resource source, monster habitat, and pass/road challenge.

generation.possibility: Appears at the upper elevation tail of land terrain.

surfaceEffects.effect: white cap texture and cold hazard

physicalBehavior.movement: Very slow unless following passes or roads.
physicalBehavior.control: Steep control; falls matter.

methods.notes: Only large projects can carve a mountain surface.

rules.realWorldDescription: Snowfields are cold alpine or polar surfaces with seasonal or persistent snow cover over ground.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Cold travel terrain, readable visually, and a bridge between mountains, tundra, and glacier.

generation.possibility: Appears where elevation is high and temperature is low.

surfaceEffects.effect: soft bright layer that slows movement

physicalBehavior.movement: Slow without snow gear.
physicalBehavior.control: Slippery and momentum-heavy.

methods.notes: Snow melts to reveal upland ground.

rules.realWorldDescription: Tundra is cold treeless terrain, often high-latitude or alpine, with permafrost, moss, sedge, and low shrubs.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Sparse cold land for hardy creatures, low settlements, and seasonal travel.

generation.possibility: Appears in cold land that is not high enough or snowy enough for alpine snowfield.

physicalBehavior.movement: Moderate when frozen, slow when thawed.
physicalBehavior.control: Uneven frost hummocks.

rules.realWorldDescription: Savanna is warm seasonal grassland with scattered trees, shaped by wet/dry seasons, grazing, and fire.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Open warm biome for herds, speed, fire risk, and sparse shade.

generation.possibility: Appears in warm regions with moderate but seasonal moisture.

surfaceEffects.effect: yellow grass and high fire spread
fresh green texture and more creatures

physicalBehavior.movement: Fast, especially mounted.
physicalBehavior.control: Stable except during fire or heavy rain.

rules.realWorldDescription: Rainforest is warm, very humid, closed-canopy forest with layered vegetation and high biodiversity.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Dense high-biomass terrain, slow and dangerous but rich in creatures and rare resources.

generation.possibility: Appears in the warmest and wettest land cells.

physicalBehavior.movement: Slow without cut paths.
physicalBehavior.control: Dense vegetation reduces line of sight and turning.

rules.realWorldDescription: Marsh is flat wetland dominated by herbaceous plants, reeds, peat, and shallow standing water.
rules.alohayoBehavior: The wettest lowland terrain, excellent for amphibious creatures and very poor for roads.

generation.possibility: Appears in very moist warm lowlands.
conditions: near lake, river, coast, or basin

physicalBehavior.movement: Very slow on foot; raised paths needed.
physicalBehavior.control: Sticky, unstable footing.

rules.realWorldDescription: Plateaus are elevated broad flat regions, often bounded by scarps, canyons, or escarpments.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Large elevated travel surfaces with dramatic edges, sparse settlements, and road passes.

generation.possibility: Appears where elevation is high but ruggedness remains low.

physicalBehavior.movement: Moderate on top; cliffs at edges block routes.
physicalBehavior.control: Stable on top, dangerous near scarps.

rules.realWorldDescription: Canyonlands are deeply incised dry terrain with gorges, mesas, buttes, cliffs, and narrow river corridors.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Maze-like dry terrain with choke points, bridges, hidden paths, and dramatic vertical barriers.

generation.possibility: Appears in rugged dry elevated regions.

physicalBehavior.movement: Slow unless following canyon floors or roads.
physicalBehavior.control: Edges and narrow turns constrain movement.

methods.notes: Only long geologic or magical filling smooths it.

rules.realWorldDescription: Reefs are warm shallow marine structures built by coral or rock, often forming lagoons, atolls, and hazards for ships.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Rare warm shallow-water terrain with high creature density, bright visuals, and navigation risk.

generation.possibility: Appears in warm shallow seas with a seeded reef chance.
conditions: away from cold or muddy runoff

physicalBehavior.movement: Blocked on foot; slow and careful for boats.
physicalBehavior.control: Sharp reef edges damage careless craft.

rules.realWorldDescription: An oasis is a localized water and vegetation pocket in arid terrain, often fed by groundwater or springs.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Rare desert refuge, settlement seed, road node, and creature concentration point.

generation.possibility: Rare chance inside hot dry desert cells at suitable elevation.

physicalBehavior.movement: Easy inside the green pocket; surrounding desert remains slow.
physicalBehavior.control: Stable near water, soft at wet sand edges.

methods.trigger: spring dries or well is poisoned

methods.notes: Managed water grows a broader green zone.

rules.realWorldDescription: Volcanic terrain includes cones, caldera rims, ash fields, lava flows, and geothermal ground from active or young volcanism.
rules.alohayoBehavior: High-danger rare terrain with heat, ash, unique minerals, monsters, and future eruption events.

generation.possibility: Very rare; requires high elevation, ruggedness, and a seeded hotspot.

surfaceEffects.effect: gray ash layer over nearby surfaces
black rock with red cracks and heat hazard

physicalBehavior.movement: Slow and dangerous; roads are rare and unstable.
physicalBehavior.control: Rubble and heat vents force detours.

methods.notes: Long-lived volcanic soil supports upland cover.

rules.realWorldDescription: Glaciers are persistent land ice masses that form where snow accumulation exceeds melt and slowly flow downhill.
rules.alohayoBehavior: Rare cold barrier and water source for future rivers, caves, crevasses, and ancient discoveries.

generation.possibility: Very rare; requires very cold, wet, high terrain.

physicalBehavior.movement: Very slow and slippery without gear.
physicalBehavior.control: Sliding inertia and poor braking.

methods.notes: Meltwater can leave a proglacial lake.
